title: My Experiences
text: Over the years I've worked in pretty different environments — NGOs, fintechs, big media companies, US corporations, and freelance. Some roles were about leading teams and owning technical decisions, others were about shipping fast as an individual contributor. The variety pushed me to adapt constantly, and that's probably where I grew the most.
subtitle: My experience as a developer

experiences.text: At Fox I worked as a senior frontend engineer on a smart TV streaming app built with Remix and React Router 7. I joined to take the project from zero to launch — handling architecture, implementation, and cross-platform support across Tizen, WebOS, Vizio, and Comcast TVs. I also integrated AI-assisted tooling into the development workflow to speed up some of the more repetitive parts of the build.

Globoplay is the streaming platform of Globo, Brazil's biggest TV network, with millions of active users. Building for Smart TVs pushed me to think differently about performance — the constraints are real and the tolerance for lag is zero. Managing cross-manufacturer compatibility while keeping everything snappy was one of the hardest technical challenges I've worked through.

Short-term contract to modernize a series of microservices — but the scope ended up being substantial. I upgraded a 10-year-old codebase from Node.js 12 to 24 and MongoDB 4 to 8, refactored it to run in modern Docker environments, and migrated version control and packages from AWS CodeArtifact to GitHub. I also automated deployment workflows with GitHub Actions and leaned on AI coding tools throughout to move faster on the more tedious parts of the migration.

World Pulse is an NGO based in Portland, Oregon. I came in as tech lead and owned the whole thing — picking the technologies, setting up the infrastructure, and managing the team day-to-day. We built the platform from scratch using NextJS, GraphQL, Directus, Postgres, and Strapi. Beyond the technical side, I ran sprint planning, reviewed PRs, and handled all releases.

Three years building a multi-tenant platform for car dealerships from the ground up. At our peak we had over 150 live sites running on it. This is where I got serious about web performance — every site hit 100% on Google Lighthouse across all categories, and they all ranked on Google's first page. I handled the full stack: Laravel and MariaDB on the backend, Node and Express on the API layer, and all the designs too.

Freelancing taught me how to adapt fast. I've built everything from landing pages to full applications, usually working independently across the full stack. It's where I learned to ship things quickly, make pragmatic decisions, and communicate clearly with non-technical clients.

In 2019 my team built Bari's banking website using NextJS — one of the very first projects we'd done with it. Back then the framework was still niche and the community was tiny. It was an early bet that paid off.
